attributes.description: Fires when the worst mountpoint (by used bytes vs used+free) for each host exceeds 85% in the lookback. Uses `system.filesystem.usage` for `used` and `free` states. Tune 85.0 in ESQL to match your capacity policy.
attributes.name: [System Host Metrics OTel] Filesystem space above critical threshold

investigation_guide.blob:
## Disk space

1. Identify `mountpoint` in dashboard drilldown; extend volumes or clean logs/cache.
2. If near full on a DB volume, also check `system.filesystem.inodes` rule.
3. `reserved` state is not included; adjust formula if you must account for root reserve.

**Tune** Change `85.0` to your capacity threshold (e.g. 90).

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-hostmetricsreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE `metrics.system.filesystem.usage` IS NOT NULL
| WHERE state IN ("used", "free")
| STATS u = MAX(CASE(state == "used", `metrics.system.filesystem.usage`, null)), f = MAX(CASE(state == "free", `metrics.system.filesystem.usage`, null)) BY `host.name`, mountpoint
| WHERE u IS NOT NULL AND f IS NOT NULL
| EVAL total_b = u + f
| WHERE total_b > 0
| EVAL used_pct = u / to_double(total_b) * 100.0
| WHERE used_pct > 85.0
| SORT used_pct DESC
| STATS worst_used_pct = MAX(used_pct) BY `host.name`
| WHERE worst_used_pct > 85.0
| SORT worst_used_pct DESC
| LIMIT 20
